key_insight: Graph neural networks are now a general-purpose choice for relational data, but their real value depends on when graph structure truly justifies the added computation.
engineering_relevance: For materials discovery and other engineering domains, the paper frames GNN success around graph construction quality, expressive power, and deployment constraints rather than leaderboard scores alone.
method: Unifies spectral and spatial GNN views from shared first principles
Connects model expressiveness to the Weisfeiler-Leman hierarchy
Compares 12 application domains using architecture choice, graph construction cost, and baseline quality
evidence_signal: The survey synthesizes cross-domain results and explicitly separates reported gains from weak baselines or favorable data splits.
watch_item: Heterophily, temporal dynamics, and distribution shift often reduce GNN effectiveness, so strong offline results may not transfer cleanly to deployment.
paper_title: Graph Neural Networks Applications Across Domains: All Insights You Need
